title: malty crispy energizing portuguese peas paella
description: A twist on traditional paella, this dish combines the sweetness of malty flavors with the crunch of crispy texture, all featuring fresh peas and a delicious ovo-vegetarian-friendly omelette.

ingredients.name: Portuguese chorizo
ingredients.notes: use mild sausage for less heat

ingredients.name: Malty beer
ingredients.notes: use a dark, malty beer like a porter or stout

instructions: Heat the oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chorizo and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it's browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until they're softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika and cayenne pepper, if using, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the peas, rice, and beer, stirring to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
While the paella cooks, heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat.
Pour in the beaten eggs and cook, stirring occasionally, until the omelette is set.
Cut the omelette into wedges and set aside.
Once the paella is done cooking, remove it from the heat and let it rest for 5 minutes.
To serve, place a spoonful of the paella in a bowl and top with a wedge of the omelette and a sprinkle of smoked paprika.
